title: page-html stage never reads the outline — propagate deck-global data via planning JSON
body: The page-HTML stage (tpl-page-html.md) reads only per-page planning JSON + style.json; it does NOT read the outline. So any deck-global datum an outline field introduces (e.g. persistent_chrome, and the deck_chrome copy it needs) must be recorded into planning JSON by the planning stage (tpl-page-planning.md, which does read the outline) to reach page-html. Deck-global values also fan out: each per-page planning agent reads 'only your page', so the deck-global read must be marked mandatory-on-every-page or one page silently drops it.

title: planning playbook enums must track planning_validator.py — a harness enforces it
body: The Step 4 page-planning playbook (page-planning-playbook.md, injected verbatim into the planning subagent via tpl-page-planning.md) is the contract the subagent uses to emit planning.json. Every enum, budget cap, and structural rule it states must match scripts/planning_validator.py, which is the single source of truth (zero-ERROR = compliant). When you add or change a VALID_* set, DENSITY_DEFAULTS value, or result.error() site in the validator, update the playbook's matching guidance in lockstep. tests/test_planning_schema_compliance.py enforces this: it imports the validator's own constants, proves each of the N result.error() sites both fires and has a playbook/prompt anchor, validates a golden zero-ERROR deck, and has a bijective coverage check (distinct emitting source lines == count of result.error() calls) that fails if you add an unguarded error site. Two live defects this caught: visual_weight was documented 1-10 but the validator caps at 9, and the copy-paste card skeletons hardcoded body_max_lines:5 which exceeds the low/mid_low/high/dashboard density caps.
